what_it_does: This file is a delivery/status note for a research synthesis about the V-surface of an application, listing the nine sections it contains, the load-bearing corrections applied, and the verification claims made against the live repository.
observations: It claims to be a completed synthesis in loop-prep house style: provisional, honest-status, resolution-first, and expansive.

It enumerates nine requested parts: type/registry model, reuse-vs-build dimensions, mode-colour cascade, slot/socket expansion, channels/RHM/common-memory, decisions-as-slides, gallery-app principles, BEYOND items, and a seam/risk table plus end-to-end circuit.

It records several corrections: a composition runtime and component.schema.json are unreachable, so V-slot-socket work is re-tiered from BUILT to DESIGNED; mode is treated as an anti-exemplar while window/mark_type is the exemplar; a scheme-ordering invariant is added; decision write-back is corrected to write a mark; and an internal §10/R7 contradiction is resolved by scoping mode changes to BEYOND.
It references specific files such as tokens.json, palette.js, brand-icon.js, and component.schema.json.
It includes a DONE marker and claims the synthesis was written to a specific absolute path.
standouts: The file foregrounds 'load-bearing corrections' rather than hiding them, including re-tiering work because the build cannot edit files on an unreachable filesystem.
It resolves an internal contradiction by stating that mode changes belong in BEYOND rather than issuing a premature 'fix mode first' directive.
It explicitly names mode as the anti-exemplar and window/mark_type as the real exemplar for the type/registry model.
conventions: Follows a heavily sectioned, cross-referenced style using §1–§10, S1–S32, B1–B8, and R1–R7 markers.
Uses loop-prep house style markers: provisional, honest-status, resolution-first, expansive.
Includes a 'Done' status and self-referential verification claims.
Mixes design terminology (V-surface, MAP, RHM, BEYOND) without defining them locally.
concerns: The file makes strong verification claims ('re-verified against the live repo this session') but provides no inline evidence, diffs, or hashes.
It is dense with internal jargon and cross-references whose meaning depends on external documents.
It describes a resolved contradiction but does not show the original conflicting statements or the reasoning steps.
The '9 requested parts' are listed as present, but the actual section bodies are not included in this file.
notes: A self-aware build-prep status note that catalogs what a larger V-surface synthesis now says and which corrections changed its conclusions.
questions: What exactly is the V-surface, MAP, RHM, and BEYOND in this project?
What is 'the-one-application' and where is the full synthesis text located?
Which criteria or guide now owns the deferred ordering decision?
What was the original §10/R7 contradiction text and how was it caught?
Where precisely do palette.js and brand-icon.js live, and why are they in counterpart rather than company?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a completed nine-part synthesis; actual content is a compact delivery/status summary asserting the synthesis exists and listing its contents and corrections, not the full expanded synthesis itself.
apparent_themes: Reuse versus build governed by filesystem reachability
Type/registry governance with exemplars and anti-exemplars
Cross-project common memory and channels integration
Seam and risk cataloguing as a first-class activity
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to close a research loop by documenting corrected findings, asserting verification, and signaling that contradictions have been resolved so implementation planning can proceed.
novelty: It treats design corrections as load-bearing first-class facts, uses an anti-exemplar concept to clarify type/registry modeling, and resolves a sequencing contradiction by scoping an item to BEYOND rather than issuing a premature directive.
contribution: A structured, self-aware research-synthesis status artifact that records which findings were corrected, which assumptions were re-tiered, and which decisions were deferred, serving as an audit trail for build-prep handoff.
summary_for_embedding: A build-prep status note summarizing a completed V-surface research synthesis for an application, listing its nine sections, load-bearing corrections, and verification claims.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to record and communicate the resolved state of a V-surface research synthesis before implementation planning.
